q: Do you work or are you a student?
examiner_note: Đây là câu hỏi xuất hiện ở đầu hầu hết các bài thi IELTS Speaking Part 1. Bạn nên trả lời rõ ràng tình trạng hiện tại của mình (Đang đi làm hay đang đi học). Hãy mở rộng câu trả lời bằng cách nói về chuyên ngành (major) hoặc chức danh công việc (job title), và cảm nhận của bạn về nó. Các cụm từ như 'pursue a degree', 'working professional', 'career path' sẽ rất hữu ích.

band7.text: Currently, I’m a student at National Economics University. I’m majoring in Marketing, which I find very interesting because it allows me to be creative. I’ve been studying there for about two years now and I really enjoy the academic environment.
band7.note: Trả lời trực tiếp, nêu được trường học và chuyên ngành. Có lý do tại sao lại thích chuyên ngành đó. Sử dụng từ vựng ổn.

band8.text: I’m currently a working professional. I hold the position of a Software Engineer at a leading tech firm in the city. I’ve been in this role for nearly three years, and while it can be quite demanding at times, I find the problem-solving aspect of my career path incredibly rewarding.
band8.note: Sử dụng từ vựng chuyên nghiệp (working professional, hold the position, tech firm, demanding, rewarding). Cách dẫn đạt trôi chảy và tự nhiên.

band9.text: At present, I’m a postgraduate student pursuing a Master’s degree in Applied Linguistics. I decided to further my education to gain a deeper theoretical understanding of language acquisition. It’s an intellectually stimulating environment that challenges my perspectives. However, I also have some experience as a part-time language instructor, which allows me to bridge the gap between academic theory and practical application. I feel this combination is instrumental in my long-term career aspirations.
band9.note: Sử dụng vốn từ vựng cực kỳ phong phú và học thuật (postgraduate, pursuing, theoretical understanding, language acquisition, intellectually stimulating, practical application, instrumental, aspirations). Cách lập luận vô cùng tinh tế.

v.meaning: người đang đi làm chuyên nghiệp
v.example: As a working professional, managing your time between work and life is crucial.

v.meaning: theo đuổi một tấm bằng/việc học
v.example: She chose to pursue a degree in Architecture because of her love for design.
Choosing a career path in medicine requires a long-term commitment.
The conference provided an intellectually stimulating environment for all researchers.

v.meaning: sự hiểu biết về mặt lý thuyết
v.example: A strong theoretical understanding is necessary before you start practical experiments.
Internships are an excellent way to bridge the gap between university and the workplace.
Her career aspirations include becoming a senior manager in a global firma.

v.meaning: đóng vai trò quan trọng/là phương tiện để
v.example: His guidance was instrumental in the team's success.
I really missed the academic environment after working for five years.
I decided to major in Finance to better understand the economy.
She is a postgraduate student researching renewable energy.
Early childhood is the most effective period for natural language acquisition.
A career in law can be very demanding but also very lucrative.
He has held the position of CEO for over ten years.
Most people look for full-time employment for financial stability.
Students often take a part-time job to cover their living expenses.
A summer internship is a great way to gain professional experience.
The school curriculum is updated every year to keep up with industry trends.
The senior lecturer gave a fascinating talk on artificial intelligence.
The faculty of Arts and Sciences is the largest in the university.
I need to finish my history assignment by Friday.
Meeting the deadline is crucial for the project’s success.
He is currently writing his doctoral dissertation on environmental law.
She won a full scholarship to study at Oxford University.

v.meaning: vị trí dành cho người mới vào nghề
v.example: Most graduates start their careers in an entry-level position.
Hard work and dedication are the keys to getting a promotion.

v.meaning: sự cân bằng giữa công việc và cuộc sống
v.example: Many companies are now prioritizing work-life balance for their employees.

v.meaning: sự hài lòng trong công việc
v.example: Salary is important, but job satisfaction matters even more.
It's important to find a company whose corporate culture matches your values.
Technology has made remote work a viable option for many professionals.

v.meaning: làm việc tại nhà (kết nối qua internet)
v.example: Telecommuting can save hours of time spent on daily commutes.

v.meaning: xây dựng mối quan hệ/kết nối
v.example: Networking is essential for discovering new career opportunities.

v.meaning: kỹ năng mềm (giao tiếp, làm việc nhóm...)
v.example: Communication and leadership are key soft skills in the workplace.

v.meaning: kỹ năng cứng (kiến thức chuyên môn)
v.example: Hard skills like programming and accounting are easily measurable.
The young entrepreneur started his first company at eighteen.
Working in a startup can be risky but very exciting.
Employees are entitled to twenty days of paid vacation leave per year.

v.meaning: sự dư thừa nhân viên (bị sa thải do công ty thu hẹp)
v.example: Many people faced redundancy during the economic recession.
The executive position comes with a very attractive salary package.
One of the perks of the job is free health insurance.
The company gave all employees a year-end bonus for their hard work.
Stress is an occupational hazard for many healthcare workers.
I have my annual performance appraisal with my manager tomorrow.
She worked long hours to climb the career ladder quickly.
The students are burning the midnight oil to prepare for their exams.

v.meaning: người đa năng (biết mỗi thứ một ít)
v.example: In a small company, you have to be a jack of all trades.

v.word: keep your nose to the grindstone
v.example: If you keep your nose to the grindstone, you will surely succeed.
We need someone who can think outside the box to solve this puzzle.
Internships are vital for gaining practical experience in your field.
Vocational training focus on developing specific skills for a trade.
